file_title: Direct Sales Transition: Driving Elexion's Future
introduction: As we consider a strategic shift from dealership franchising to direct-to-customer, it's crucial we understand potential benefits and key steps to drive sales and higher margins. This presentation will provide insights on how this transition could shape Elexion's future in 2027.

subsections.heading: Current State of Elexion's Sales Operations
subsections.content:
• Our sales process currently involves a 42-day journey from initial customer contact to final sale closure.
• This period, as recorded in January 2025, is representative of the average time it takes for our dealership network to finalize a sale.
• Our sales operations through the dealer network require this considerable time investment, potentially impacting customer satisfaction and sales efficiency.
• A shift to a direct sales model could potentially reduce this period, streamlining our sales process and enhancing customer experience.
• Considering the reported extra costs added by dealerships, a direct model could also provide financial advantages by eliminating middleman markups.
• This strategic shift might provide us with a competitive edge, especially considering the significant annual revenues of top dealership groups in America.
subsections.justification: These points detail current operations, indicate potential improvements and set the stage for assessing the benefits of a transition to direct sales.

subsections.heading: Potential Impact of Direct Sales on Margins
subsections.content:
• Dealership markup varies from $500 to $2,000 per vehicle
• Top 10% of dealerships account for 40% of total sales
• In 2025, dealership commission was reduced by 10%
• Annual dealership operating costs average $1.5M per location
• Dealerships also offer post-sales services and accessories
• Direct sales could alter the dynamics of after-sales services and warranty claims
subsections.justification: This slide provides information on the current cost structure of Elexion's dealership model, but not the impact of a potential shift to direct sales.

subsections.heading: Leveraging Direct Sales for Enhanced Customer Experience
subsections.content:
• Elexion's Net Promoter Score (NPS) has been above 70 since 2023
• 60% of customer complaints relate to dealerships, not the product
• In 2025, a pilot program was launched to test direct sales for fleet customers
• Elexion's customer service team handles over 10,000 queries per month
• Elexion's website traffic has increased by 25% YOY since 2024
subsections.justification: This slide discusses Elexion's current customer experience and pilot programs, but doesn't address how direct sales could enhance the customer experience.

conclusion: Transitioning to a direct-to-customer model could significantly increase our margins and improve customer experience. As we move forward, we must align our operations and strategies to leverage this potential advantage effectively.
